data.alert_description: Investigate loss of reachability from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12 (0/25 pings); determine root cause and required remediation.

recent_changes.summary: Discovered serial links: R1-R2, R1-R3, R2-R3 (from device configs).

root_causes.cause: R2 has no route for the 192.168.0.0/24 network, so ICMP replies from 172.16.0.12 to 192.168.0.12 are not being sent back via R1; instead R2 would use its default route (toward R3) or drop the traffic, resulting in 0/25 pings.
evidence: R1 has LAN 192.168.0.1/24 (GigabitEthernet0/0) and static route for 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.2 (R1 routing entry: 'prefix': '172.16.0.0' fwd-list '10.0.0.2').
R2 has LAN 172.16.0.1/24 (GigabitEthernet0/0) but its routing config shows only a default route ('prefix': '0.0.0.0' fwd-list '10.0.0.6') and no route to 192.168.0.0/24.
R1-R2 serial interface addresses are 10.0.0.1 (R1 Serial0/1/0) and 10.0.0.2 (R2 Serial0/1/0) — path exists, but R2 lacks an appropriate route back to the source network.

ACLs on R1 and R2 (R1_TO_R2 and R2_TO_R1) only deny specific TCP ports then permit ip (e.g. R1 denies tcp dst port 80 then 'permit ip any any'; R2 denies tcp dst port 443 then 'permit ip any any'), so they do not explain ICMP being blocked.

data.intent_description: Restore IP reachability so 172.16.0.12 can reply to 192.168.0.12 (0/25 pings) by correcting asymmetric/missing return route on R2.

plan.problem: R2 has no route for 192.168.0.0/24, causing ICMP replies from 172.16.0.12 to be dropped or forwarded incorrectly (0/25 pings).
plan.fix_summary: Add a static route on R2 for 192.168.0.0/24 via next-hop 10.0.0.1 (R1 Serial0/1/0).

recent_changes.summary: Discovered serial links: R1-R2, R1-R3, R2-R3 (from device configs).

evidence: Verification (most recent) — get_running_config for router2 shows a route for 192.168.0.0/24 via next-hop 10.0.0.1 (routing.ip-route-interface-forwarding-list contains entry: {"prefix": "192.168.0.0", "mask": "255.255.255.0", "fwd-list": [{"fwd": "10.0.0.1"}]}).

Router interface evidence: R2 Serial0/1/0 has IP 10.0.0.2 and R1 Serial0/1/0 has IP 10.0.0.1 (network_db and device configs), so the configured next-hop 10.0.0.1 is on the directly connected link between R2 and R1.
R2 access-list 'R2_TO_R1' contains a 'permit ip any any' ACE after a single specific TCP deny, so ICMP traffic is not being blocked by that ACL (access_lists entry in router2 config).

Verification (most recent) — get_running_config for router2 shows a route for 192.168.0.0/24 via next-hop 10.0.0.1 (routing.ip-route-interface-forwarding-list contains entry: {"prefix": "192.168.0.0", "mask": "255.255.255.0", "fwd-list": [{"fwd": "10.0.0.1"}]}).

Router interface evidence: R2 Serial0/1/0 has IP 10.0.0.2 and R1 Serial0/1/0 has IP 10.0.0.1 (network_db and device configs), so the configured next-hop 10.0.0.1 is on the directly connected link between R2 and R1.
R2 access-list 'R2_TO_R1' contains a 'permit ip any any' ACE after a single specific TCP deny, so ICMP traffic is not being blocked by that ACL (access_lists entry in router2 config).

messages.content:
What was observed
- Pings from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12 were failing (0/25). R1 had a route to 172.16.0.0/24 but R2 lacked a route back to 192.168.0.0/24.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- Missing return route on R2 (asymmetric/missing routing): R2 had no route for 192.168.0.0/24, so ICMP replies from 172.16.0.12 were not forwarded back via R1.

Changes made
- Added a static route on R2: 192.168.0.0/24 via next-hop 10.0.0.1 (R1).

Verification
- Passed. R2 running-config now contains the 192.168.0.0/24 route via 10.0.0.1; R1/R2 serial addresses confirm the next-hop is reachable; ACLs do not block ICMP. No remaining issues detected.

data.last_message:
What was observed
- Pings from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12 were failing (0/25). R1 had a route to 172.16.0.0/24 but R2 lacked a route back to 192.168.0.0/24.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- Missing return route on R2 (asymmetric/missing routing): R2 had no route for 192.168.0.0/24, so ICMP replies from 172.16.0.12 were not forwarded back via R1.

Changes made
- Added a static route on R2: 192.168.0.0/24 via next-hop 10.0.0.1 (R1).

Verification
- Passed. R2 running-config now contains the 192.168.0.0/24 route via 10.0.0.1; R1/R2 serial addresses confirm the next-hop is reachable; ACLs do not block ICMP. No remaining issues detected.
